title: Question engine core for project intake wizard
description:
Generalize the prompter pattern from `bin/new-task.js` into a reusable question engine that the upcoming `init` wizard (TASK-012) and the existing `new-task` CLI both consume. This is the first ticket in the onboarding-wizard chain (TASK-010..015) that will let a non-technical user clone the framework and have it ask them what they want to build.

The engine must:
- Accept a declarative array of questions with `{id, prompt, type, required, validate, enum, when}` fields.
- Support sequence (ordered) and branching (`when(answers)` predicate gates the next question).
- Re-prompt on validation failure with a human-readable message that names the field.
- Persist partial answers atomically after each question so a Ctrl+C / crash resumes from the next unanswered question on the next invocation â€” uses `atomicWriteFile` from src/atomic-write.js, writes to a caller-supplied path so the wizard can scope it under `state/sessions/<id>/intake.json` and the new-task CLI can opt out of persistence with `persistTo: null`.
- Be prompter-injectable so tests run with zero TTY (same pattern as `bin/new-task.js`).

As part of the same change, refactor `bin/new-task.js` to delegate to the new engine with its existing question set. This proves the generalization actually works against an existing caller and prevents two divergent prompter implementations from coexisting.

Reference: the design conversation that produced this ticket lives in the 2026-05-26 session â€” taxonomy (A) curated + escape hatch agreed, this is the first of six tickets (010 engine, 011 question library + PROJECT.md, 012 bin/init.js + routing, 013 agent generator, 014 backlog seeder, 015 UX polish).
acceptance_criteria: `src/question-engine.js` exports `runQuestionnaire({questions, prompter, persistTo, now})` returning `Promise<{answers, completedAt}>` where `answers` is keyed by question `id`.
Question shape supports `{id, prompt, type, required, validate, enum, when}` with `type` in {'string','number','enum','multi'}; `when(answers)` returning false skips that question (branching).

Validation failure (required-but-empty, regex mismatch, enum mismatch, type mismatch) triggers a re-prompt with an error message containing the question's `id` or `prompt` text; the answer is not persisted until valid.

After each accepted answer, the in-progress `{answers, lastAnsweredId}` payload is written via `atomicWriteFile` to `persistTo` (when supplied). A second `runQuestionnaire` call with the same `persistTo` reads the existing file and resumes at the question immediately after `lastAnsweredId`.
`persistTo: null` (or omitted) disables persistence entirely; no temp files appear on disk for that run.

`bin/new-task.js` is refactored to delegate to `runQuestionnaire` for its title/description/AC-count/AC/priority prompt loop; the 4 pre-existing tests in `tests/new-task-cli.spec.js` pass without modification.

New `tests/question-engine.spec.js` covers: (a) sequence with all-string questions, (b) branching via `when`, (c) validation re-prompt path, (d) resume from a half-written persist file, (e) `persistTo: null` writes nothing, (f) atomic-write call ordering (fsync before rename via `vi.mock('node:fs')`).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the 2026-05-26 design conversation. User wants the framework to be cloneable by a non-technical operator who is then walked through an intake wizard that configures agents and seeds a backlog. We rejected adopting GSD wholesale because of the .planning/ vs tasks/ state-model collision, so we are building our own intake on top of the primitives already in the repo: prompter pattern from bin/new-task.js, atomic writes from src/atomic-write.js, lifecycle bundles for resumability. This ticket is foundational â€” TASK-011 through TASK-015 all depend on the engine landing first. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering all 7 ACs, then implements + refactors bin/new-task.js to delegate, then reviewer in fresh context.
